bake_nav.description: bakes all navmesh volumes for the current level. Does not save to disk - follow with save_baked_nav to persist.

bake_probes.description: bakes all static GI cubemap probes for the current level. Does not save to disk - follow with save_baked_gi to persist.

create-map.description: creates a new empty .tmap at a game-relative path. Fails if the file already exists.

describe_command.description: describes a single console command/cvar by exact name (name, is_command, description, usage).

eval_lua.description: executes Lua source against the running engine's Lua state and returns its value as JSON. Tries the code as an expression first (`return <code>`), falling back to running it verbatim for statements/blocks. Errors (syntax or runtime) come back as a normal bridge error.

exec.description: runs every line of a text file as a console command, immediately (before the next queued command). Same file lookup convention as EngineVars.ini/init.txt.

export_tmap.description: exports a .tmap/.tprefab's MeshComponents to a standalone .gltf, with baked world-space transforms, for external tools (e.g. the Blender viewer addon). Reads the asset directly without spawning a Level, so it never touches any level currently open in the editor.

get_log.description: returns the tail of this process's engine log (Logs/output.log or Logs/test_<mode>_output.log), as a list of lines, newest last. Lets an agent watching over cscli see recent [Error]/[Warning]/[Debug]/[Info] output without knowing the log path or polling the file itself.

list_commands.description: lists registered console commands and cvars (optionally substring-filtered), each with whatever description/usage docs/console_commands.json has for it.

open-editor.description: opens (or switches to) the level editor tool, optionally loading a map. Requires the process to have been launched with --editor; a no-op error otherwise.
open-editor.usage: open-editor [game-relative .tmap/.tprefab path, omit for an empty scene]

ping.description: connectivity check - returns {"pong": true} if the bridge is alive.

run_command.description: runs a console command line exactly as if typed at the in-game console, and returns everything it printed. `had_error` is true if any part of the line logged at Error level.

status.description: reports which process (editor or game), pid, project base, and agent bridge port this bridge instance belongs to. Used by `cscli status`/`cscli instances` to identify a connection.
